steps.title: Isolate j by multiplying both sides by 5
steps.content:
We have $\frac{j}{5}=k+9m$. To isolate $j$, multiply both sides by 5:
$$5 \cdot \frac{j}{5} = 5 \cdot (k+9m)$$$$j = 5(k+9m)$$This is exactly choice B — B is correct.

steps.title: Understanding the common mistakes
steps.content:
The key is treating $(k+9m)$ as a single unit when multiplying by 5. Compare the choices:
  • A: $j=k+5(9m)$ — incorrectly applies the 5 only to $9m$
  • C: $j=5k+9m$ — incompletely distributes, missing the coefficient on $9m$
  • D: $j=\frac{k+9m}{5}$ — reverses the operation (divides instead of multiplies)
